gaps: An era-aware base-rate cut (e.g. stats bucketed pre-1925 vs post-1925) — --term/--date-from cannot slice historical cases whose corpus rows have null dates and empty docket numbers.

A read-only way to see the already-ingested opinion-cluster metadata (citation, decision date, disposition text) for the case's own linked cluster; the snapshot's bare cluster URL is the single most informative fact and I could not use it.
helpful: fedcourts stats --group-by disposition gave the decisive calibration anchor (78% of resolved SCOTUS cases in the corpus land on `other`).
fedcourts query showed the nearest priors are the same species of sparse historical docket, confirming the base rate applies.
notes: Snapshot had no docket entries or dates, so the prediction is essentially base-rate-driven plus era reasoning from the caption and cluster link.
